reasoning: The Lean functor `F : Natᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` correctly encodes F_n := k[x]/(x^n) via `k[X] ⧸ Ideal.span {X^n}`, with transition morphisms given by `Ideal.Quotient.factor` derived from `pow_dvd_pow`, which are precisely the canonical quotient maps k[x]/(x^{n+1}) → k[x]/(x^n) specified informally. The cone `cone_F k` has apex `PowerSeries k` (= k[[x]]) with cone maps given by `PowerSeries.trunc n` — the natural truncation maps. The theorem asserts `Nonempty (IsLimit (cone_F k))`, i.e., this specific cone is a limit cone. The informal claim "the limit exists and is isomorphic to k[[x]]" in context naturally means k[[x]] with truncation maps is the limit; picking these canonical maps is the standard reading rather than an added strength. Being a limit cone entails both that the limit exists and that its apex is k[[x]]. The functor direction, ordinal indexing, and cone maps all match faithfully.
